title: Types of GBP
content_md:
# Types of GBP

- Code: 7a06ff4d
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7a06ff4df2bb44f28f8f2b2aeba3a1a7
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/25c648e73e8a4451a4fd25fd9ae821ac?sid=32592a70-a97d-44f5-bb09-83fb06773567?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 6m 17s

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So let's talk about the different types of Google business profiles or GVPs. So the first thing just to make sure we're using the same terminology
These were called Google My Business listings for like a decade. So you'll hear a lot of people still call them GMBs
Okay, a GMB and a GBP are exactly the same
Google just changed the name to Google Business Profile a few years ago. Now, if there's a search that has a map result
So this is typically going to be a local search
60 to 70% of the customers of the traffic of the leads of the calls are going to go to one of the three GVPs
Okay, now on top of that
We have five to ten percent of the traffic going to the paid ads
That means that the organic results below the GVPs best case scenario ranking number one
You're only playing for 20 to 25% of the total number of people who did that search
most of the people aren't going to the GVPs and
What's important to note is in that map results right right here in this map results
These are not the businesses website. These are the businesses Google Business Profile
They're GVPs if you don't have a GVP you can't show up here
So everything we're doing in local is being done with the goal to rank the GVP higher. We're not trying to rank the website higher
We're not trying to rank URLs higher. We're trying to rank GVPs higher. Okay, so
Ranking the GVPs. How does Google do it? There are three core factors trust relevance and proximity
Now when we say trust that's coded language. It basically means links links from third parties
The more trustworthy those third-party websites are when they link to you the more relevant
They are the better those links are so when we talk about building trust with links
This is where we're going to join the Chamber of Commerce. We're going to sponsor the nine-year-old girls softball team
We're going to sponsor the Bayou Cajun bash whatever it is
We're going to sponsor a local nonprofit organization
Basically anything around us that is hyperlocal that has a powerful website and most nonprofits have one
We're going to sponsor that in exchange for a link. That's what we're trying to do to build this trust now relevance
We build relevance with content
This is where we're going to build geographic relevance and we're going to build topical relevance now
We are sourcing links to this relevance content, but we're not sourcing these powerful links, right?
The links that we source here they're designed to get that content indexed to get Google to recognize that it's quality content
That is not AI slop the links that we build to this content are different from these links that are designed to build trust
Okay, keep that in mind and the third one of course is proximity the farther away
The searcher is from your GVP address the harder it's going to be to rank
That's why you'll very often see rank maps where one two three near the center and they drop off as you're farther and farther away
Now we can overcome that we can overcome proximity
We can outrank GVPs that are closer to the searcher if we've built enough trust and enough relevance
And that's essentially what we're doing in a local SEO engagement
We're trying to build trust and relevance so that we rank higher farther and farther away from the GVP address
Okay, now I mentioned organic briefly before when I say organic
I of course mean the first page of results the top ten for local search
They appear below the maps you may have heard my favorite joke in SEO
It's the only one I know where's the best place to hide a dead body the second page of Google search
So the second page of Google search is number 11 for organic
But for a local SEO for a GVP the fourth result is the first result on the second page
Now what's important to that is
That if you are ranked number four you're getting roughly the same one of traffic as if you were ranked number 80, okay?
If you don't do enough SEO to get into the top three save your money and don't do any at all
Okay, there's no benefit from moving from 80 to position five
You're gonna get the same amount of traffic you got to get into the top three or don't bother
So let's talk more broadly about the overall types of GVPs first is a single location business
So this is a local business. They have one GVP
They have a website the GVP landing page, which is the URL in the GVP for the website box
That's going to point almost always to the home page home page is the most powerful URL on that domain
So using that as your landing page will get you to rank faster
We also have a multi location business now a multi location business that means you have one website and multiple GVPs
You do not want each GVP pointing to the home page each GVP should have an independent
GVP landing page the reason for that is the landing page needs to be optimized for that specific GVP
We want to use local business schema. We want to embed the GVP. We want to embed reviews
We want to optimize the content for the city that GVP is located in we can't optimize one landing page for multiple GVPs
But if you're taking on a new client and they have a multi location business and they have multiple GVPs
All pointed to the home page before you change anything run a rank map, okay?
I've had clients come to me before they had eight locations all eight were pointed to the home page
Six of them were ranking really well and two of them weren't so the two that weren't ranking well
We created new landing pages for those two. We left the other six alone. Don't touch it if it's ranking
Okay, that's a rule for SEOs to live by and the third one of course is the service area business
Do not use the service area business. Okay using a service area business is is awful. It's terrible
It it takes 30 to 50 percent more effort to rank a service area business than it does a visible address
So to put this in numbers if you estimate a $3,000 per month engagement to rank a visible address
If that instead where you service area business you'd be looking at spending significantly more to rank them as a service area business
Do not
Take on a service area business unless you and the client are crystal clear on the downsides of a service area business
It is usually less expensive to get office space and have a visible address
Than it is to do an SEO engagement on a service area business
Okay, make sure that is very very well understood before embarking on an SAP local SEO engagement

summary: The lesson explains different types of Google Business Profiles (GBP) and emphasizes that local SEO efforts should focus on ranking these profiles higher. It discusses single location, multi-location, and service area businesses, highlighting the importance of optimizing landing pages for each type.
key_points: Focus on ranking GVPs higher in organic search results
Optimize landing pages for specific locations
Avoid service area businesses due to increased effort required
